recipes.title: Μουσακάς
recipes.description: Iconic layered casserole of eggplants, ground lamb, and creamy béchamel sauce — the flagship dish of Greek cuisine.
instructions: Slice eggplants into 1cm slices, salt and let sit for 30 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry and fry in olive oil until golden.
Fry onion in olive oil until translucent, add garlic and lamb and cook until meat browns.
Add tomato paste, wine, cinnamon, salt and pepper. Cook 15 minutes on low heat until sauce thickens.
Make béchamel: melt butter, mix in flour, and gradually add milk while stirring. Season with nutmeg and add egg.
Arrange in baking dish in layers — eggplant, meat, eggplant — and pour béchamel on top. Bake at 180°C for about 45 minutes until golden.

recipes.title: Σουβλάκι Κοτόπουλο
recipes.description: Juicy chicken skewers marinated in lemon and oregano, served in warm pita with tzatziki and vegetables.
instructions: Cut chicken breast into cubes (about 3cm). Mix lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Add chicken cubes to marinade, cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (or overnight for best results).
Thread chicken onto skewers. Grill over high heat or in a grill pan for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally.
Warm pita bread. Serve skewers with tzatziki, chopped tomatoes, onions, and lettuce. Can be wrapped in pita.

recipes.title: Αυγολέμονο
recipes.description: Silky chicken soup with egg-lemon sauce — comfort in a bowl, Greek style.

ingredients.name: salt and white pepper to taste

instructions: In a pot, bring chicken broth to a boil. Add rice and cook 15 minutes until tender.
In a bowl, beat eggs well. Slowly add lemon juice while whisking.
Temper the egg mixture: slowly add 1-2 ladles of hot broth while whisking constantly (this prevents curdling).
Remove soup from heat. Slowly pour egg-lemon mixture into soup while stirring constantly.
Return to very low heat for 2 minutes without boiling. Season with salt and white pepper. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Σπανακόπιτα
recipes.description: Crispy phyllo pie filled with fresh spinach, feta cheese, and aromatic herbs — a classic Greek pastry.
instructions: Sauté chopped onion in olive oil until soft. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Let cool.
In a bowl, mix cooled spinach, crumbled feta, beaten eggs, dill, salt, and pepper.
Brush a 9x13 inch baking dish with melted butter. Layer 6 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Spread spinach-feta mixture evenly. Cover with 6 more phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Score the top into squares. Bake at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden and crispy.

recipes.title: Μπακλαβάς
recipes.description: Layers of crispy phyllo, chopped nuts, and honey syrup — the most iconic Greek dessert.
instructions: Mix chopped walnuts, pistachios, cinnamon, and sugar.
Brush a 9x13 inch pan with melted butter. Layer 8 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Sprinkle a layer of nut mixture. Add 2 phyllo sheets (buttered), then nuts. Repeat until nuts are used.
Top with 8 phyllo sheets, buttering each. Cut into diamond or square shapes before baking.
Bake at 160°C for 50-60 minutes until golden. While hot, pour cooled syrup (honey, water, lemon) over. Let absorb overnight.

recipes.title: Χωριάτικη Σαλάτα
recipes.description: Authentic village salad with chunky vegetables, olives, and a slab of feta — no lettuce, pure Greek flavors.
instructions: Cut tomatoes into large wedges, cucumber into thick half-moons, and bell pepper into wide strips.
Slice red onion into thick rings. Arrange vegetables on a platter.
Top with Kalamata olives and a thick slab (not crumbled) of feta cheese.
Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil. Sprinkle with dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Do not toss. Serve immediately with crusty bread to soak up the juices.

recipes.title: Παστίτσιο
recipes.description: Greek pasta bake with layers of tubular pasta, spiced meat sauce, and creamy béchamel — comfort food at its finest.
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions until al dente. Drain and mix with beaten egg and grated cheese.
Brown ground beef with onion and garlic. Add tomato sauce, wine, cinnamon, oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer 20 minutes.
Make béchamel: melt butter, add flour, gradually whisk in milk. Cook until thick. Season with nutmeg and stir in egg yolks.
In a baking dish, layer half the pasta, all the meat sauce, remaining pasta, and top with béchamel.
Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until golden. Let rest 15 minutes before cutting.

recipes.title: Φασολάδα
recipes.description: Hearty white bean soup with vegetables and herbs — considered the national dish of Greece.
instructions: Soak white beans overnight. Drain and rinse.
In a large pot, sauté onion, carrots, and celery in olive oil until soft.
Add beans, tomato paste, bay leaves, and water. Bring to boil, then simmer 1.5-2 hours until beans are tender.
Add tomatoes, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook another 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaves. Drizzle with quality olive oil before serving. Serve with crusty bread and olives.

recipes.title: Τυρόπιτα
recipes.description: Cheese pie with phyllo and mixed Greek cheeses — a savory breakfast or snack.

ingredients.name: freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Mix crumbled feta, ricotta, grated kasseri (or mozzarella), beaten eggs, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Brush a 9x13 inch pan with melted butter. Layer 6 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Spread cheese mixture evenly over phyllo.
Cover with 6 more phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter. Score the top into squares.
Bake at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden and crispy. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Λουκουμάδες
recipes.description: Greek honey puffs — crispy fried dough balls drizzled with honey and sprinkled with cinnamon and nuts.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water with a pinch of sugar. Let sit 10 minutes until foamy.
Mix flour, salt, and yeast mixture. Add water gradually to form a thick, sticky batter. Cover and let rise 1 hour.
Heat oil to 180°C. Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil. Fry until golden brown, turning once (3-4 minutes total).
Drain on paper towels. Arrange on a platter while still warm.
Drizzle generously with honey. Sprinkle with cinnamon and chopped walnuts. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Φακές
recipes.description: Hearty lentil soup with vegetables and vinegar — simple, nutritious, and delicious.
instructions: Rinse lentils. In a pot, combine lentils, chopped onion, carrots, celery, garlic, bay leaves, and water.
Bring to boil, then simmer 30-40 minutes until lentils are tender.
Add tomato paste, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Cook another 10 minutes.
Remove bay leaves. Drizzle with red wine vinegar and olive oil before serving.
Serve with olives, feta cheese, and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Κακαβιά
recipes.description: Traditional Greek fisherman soup with fresh fish, vegetables, and lemon — simple and flavorful.

ingredients.name: white fish fillet (barramundi or sea bream)

instructions: In a pot, sauté onion, celery, and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add diced potatoes, tomatoes, bay leaf, and water. Simmer 15 minutes.
Add fish chunks (firm white fish). Simmer 10 minutes until fish is cooked.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Drizzle with olive oil.
Serve hot with crusty bread and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Τραχανάς
recipes.description: Rustic soup made with fermented wheat and milk granules — a comforting Greek staple.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pot. Sauté chopped onion and tomato until soft.
Add trahanas (fermented wheat granules) and stir for 1 minute.
Pour in hot water or broth, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Simmer 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and creamy.
Season with salt and pepper. Top with crumbled feta or grated cheese before serving.

recipes.title: Σπανακόρυζο
recipes.description: Light spinach and rice soup with lemon — healthy and refreshing.
instructions: Sauté chopped onion and scallions in olive oil until soft.
Add chopped spinach and cook until wilted.
Add rice and vegetable broth. Simmer 15-20 minutes until rice is tender.
Season with salt, pepper, and fresh lemon juice.
Drizzle with olive oil and serve with dill garnish.

recipes.title: Ρεβιθιά
recipes.description: Slow-baked chickpea soup with lemon and herbs — creamy, tangy, and nutritious.
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight. Drain and rinse.
In a baking dish, combine chickpeas, chopped onion, olive oil, baking soda, salt, and water.
Cover and bake at 160°C for 3-4 hours until chickpeas are very soft and creamy.
Stir occasionally, adding water if needed.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Serve hot with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Κουνουπίδι Σούπα Αυγολέμονο
recipes.description: Creamy cauliflower soup with egg-lemon sauce — velvety and comforting.
instructions: Boil cauliflower florets in vegetable broth until very tender.
Blend half the cauliflower to create a creamy base. Leave the rest chunky.
Beat eggs with lemon juice. Temper with hot soup.
Remove soup from heat and stir in egg-lemon mixture.
Season with salt and white pepper. Garnish with fresh herbs.

recipes.title: Ντοματόσουπα
recipes.description: Rich tomato soup with orzo and fresh herbs — hearty and satisfying.
instructions: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil. Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, vegetable broth, oregano, and bay leaf. Simmer 20 minutes.
Add orzo pasta and cook 10 minutes until tender.
Remove bay leaf. Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of sugar.
Drizzle with olive oil and garnish with fresh basil. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Μανιταρόσουπα
recipes.description: Creamy mushroom soup with fresh herbs and a hint of lemon — earthy and delicious.
instructions: Sauté sliced mushrooms in butter until golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion and garlic. Add flour and cook 2 minutes.
Gradually add vegetable broth, whisking to prevent lumps. Simmer 10 minutes.
Return mushrooms to pot. Add cream, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Garnish with fresh parsley or dill. Serve hot.

recipes.title: Γύρος
recipes.description: Marinated pork or chicken cooked on a vertical rotisserie, served in pita with tzatziki and vegetables.
instructions: Slice pork shoulder or chicken thighs thinly. Marinate with olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper for 4 hours.
If using a vertical rotisserie, stack marinated meat and cook. Otherwise, grill or pan-fry in batches until crispy.
Warm pita bread. Spread with tzatziki.
Fill with cooked meat, sliced tomatoes, onions, and lettuce.
Wrap tightly and serve with fries on the side (Greek style).

recipes.title: Κεφτέδες
recipes.description: Herb-packed Greek meatballs with fresh mint and oregano — crispy outside, juicy inside.

ingredients.name: stale white bread (crust removed), soaked in water

instructions: Mix ground beef, grated onion, crushed garlic, beaten egg, breadcrumbs, chopped mint, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Knead mixture well with hands. Let rest 30 minutes in refrigerator.
Form small balls (walnut-size). Roll lightly in flour.
Fry in hot olive oil until golden brown on all sides and cooked through (about 10 minutes).
Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with lemon wedges and tzatziki.

recipes.title: Κλέφτικο
recipes.description: Slow-roasted lamb with lemon and herbs, traditionally cooked in parchment — melt-in-your-mouth tender.
instructions: Marinate lamb shoulder pieces with lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, oregano, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Refrigerate 4 hours.
Place lamb in a roasting pan with sliced potatoes, onions, and tomatoes around it.
Pour marinade over everything. Add a bit of water. Cover tightly with foil or parchment paper.
Bake at 160°C for 3-4 hours until lamb is fall-apart tender.
Uncover for last 30 minutes to brown. Serve with pan juices and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Στιφάδο
recipes.description: Rich beef stew with pearl onions, tomatoes, and warm spices — slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Brown beef cubes in olive oil. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté pearl onions until golden. Add garlic, tomato paste, and wine. Simmer 2 minutes.
Return beef to pot. Add tomatoes, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, cloves, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Cover and simmer on very low heat for 2-3 hours until beef is very tender and sauce is thick.
Remove bay leaves and cinnamon stick. Serve hot with crusty bread or pasta.

recipes.title: Ψάρι Πλακί
recipes.description: Baked fish with tomatoes, onions, and herbs — a simple and healthy Greek classic.

ingredients.name: white fish fillet (sea bream, mullet, or barramundi)

instructions: Place fish fillets (or whole fish) in a baking dish. Season with salt and pepper.
Top with sliced onions, tomatoes, garlic, parsley, and olive oil.
Add white wine and a bit of water. Sprinkle with oregano.
Bake at 180°C for 30-40 minutes until fish is cooked through and vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with lemon wedges and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Κοτόπουλο Λεμονάτο
recipes.description: Lemon chicken with herbs and potatoes — juicy, tangy, and aromatic.
instructions: Marinate chicken pieces with lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper for 1 hour.
Place chicken and potato wedges in a roasting pan.
Pour marinade over. Add chicken broth or water.
Bake at 180°C for 1-1.5 hours, basting occasionally, until chicken is golden and potatoes are crispy.
Serve hot with pan juices and a fresh salad.

recipes.title: Γεμιστά
recipes.description: Tomatoes and peppers stuffed with herbed rice and baked until tender — a summer classic.
instructions: Cut tops off tomatoes and peppers, scoop out insides. Reserve pulp and chop. Lightly salt shells.
Sauté onion in olive oil. Add rice, tomato pulp, parsley, mint, dill, salt, and pepper. Cook 5 minutes.
Stuff vegetables with rice mixture (fill only 3/4 full, rice will expand). Replace tops.
Arrange in baking dish. Add potatoes around vegetables. Drizzle with olive oil, add water to 1cm depth.
Bake at 180°C for 1.5 hours until vegetables are tender and rice is cooked. Serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Πίτα
recipes.description: Soft, pillowy Greek flatbread perfect for wrapping souvlaki and gyros.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water with a pinch of sugar. Let sit 10 minutes until foamy.
Mix flour, salt, olive oil, and yeast mixture. Knead 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Let rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled.
Divide into 8 balls. Roll each into a thin circle.
Cook in a hot, dry skillet or griddle for 2-3 minutes per side until puffed and lightly charred. Keep warm wrapped in a towel.

recipes.title: Χωριάτικο Ψωμί
recipes.description: Rustic Greek village bread with a crispy crust and soft interior — perfect with olive oil and olives.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water. Mix with flour, salt, and olive oil. Knead 10 minutes.
Let rise 1.5 hours until doubled.
Shape into a round loaf. Place in a floured banneton or bowl. Let rise 45 minutes.
Score the top. Bake at 220°C for 35-40 minutes until golden brown with a hollow sound when tapped.
Cool on a wire rack before slicing.

recipes.title: Μπουρεκάκια
recipes.description: Small cheese or meat phyllo rolls — perfect finger food for parties.
instructions: For cheese filling: mix feta, ricotta, egg, and chopped mint. For meat: use cooked ground beef with spices.
Cut phyllo sheets into strips (about 3 inches wide). Brush with melted butter.
Place a spoonful of filling at one end. Roll tightly into a cigar, sealing edges with butter.
Arrange seam-side down on baking sheet. Brush tops with butter.
Bake at 180°C for 20-25 minutes until golden. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Τσουρέκι
recipes.description: Sweet braided Easter bread flavored with mahlab and mastic — soft, aromatic, and festive.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm milk with sugar. Add eggs, butter, flour, mahlab, mastic, salt, and orange zest. Knead 15 minutes.
Let rise 2 hours until doubled.
Divide into 3 strands. Braid tightly. Place on baking sheet.
Let rise 1 hour. Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with almonds and sesame seeds.
Bake at 180°C for 30-35 minutes until golden. Cool before slicing.

recipes.title: Κουλούρι
recipes.description: Sesame bread rings — a popular Greek street food breakfast.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water with sugar. Mix with flour, salt, and olive oil. Knead 10 minutes.
Let rise 1 hour until doubled.
Divide into 10 pieces. Roll each into a long rope and form into a ring.
Brush with water or honey-water. Dip in sesame seeds to coat.
Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden. Serve fresh.

recipes.title: Πρασόπιτα
recipes.description: Leek pie with phyllo and feta — a delicious vegetarian option.

ingredients.name: leeks (white and light green parts) sliced

instructions: Sauté sliced leeks in olive oil until very soft. Let cool.
Mix leeks with crumbled feta, beaten eggs, dill, salt, and pepper.
Brush a baking dish with butter. Layer 6 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Spread leek mixture evenly. Cover with 6 more phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Score the top into squares. Bake at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden.

recipes.title: Ελιόψωμο
recipes.description: Olive bread with herbs — rustic, flavorful, and perfect with cheese.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water. Mix with flour, salt, olive oil, chopped olives, and dried oregano. Knead 10 minutes.
Let rise 1.5 hours until doubled.
Shape into a loaf. Let rise 45 minutes.
Score the top. Brush with olive oil.
Bake at 200°C for 35-40 minutes until golden. Cool before slicing.

recipes.title: Καλτσούνια
recipes.description: Cretan cheese pastries with honey — sweet or savory, depending on the filling.
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, olive oil, raki (or water), and a pinch of salt. Knead until smooth. Rest 30 minutes.
Make filling: mix mizithra or ricotta cheese with egg, sugar (for sweet) or herbs (for savory).
Roll dough thin. Cut into circles. Place filling in center.
Fold into half-moons or leave open-faced. Seal edges.
Bake at 180°C for 20-25 minutes until golden. Drizzle sweet version with honey.

recipes.title: Γαλακτομπούρεκο
recipes.description: Custard-filled phyllo pastry soaked in honey syrup — a luscious dessert that melts in your mouth.
instructions: Make custard: heat milk with sugar and semolina, stirring constantly until thick. Remove from heat.
Beat eggs and vanilla. Slowly add to warm custard while whisking. Let cool slightly.
Butter a 9x13 inch pan. Layer 8 phyllo sheets, brushing each with melted butter.
Pour custard over phyllo. Cover with 8 more phyllo sheets, buttering each. Score the top.
Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until golden. Pour cooled syrup (sugar, water, lemon, honey) over hot pastry. Let absorb 4 hours.

recipes.title: Ραβανί
recipes.description: Moist semolina cake soaked in citrus syrup — a simple yet elegant Greek dessert.
instructions: Beat eggs with sugar until light and fluffy. Add yogurt, semolina, flour, baking powder, and vanilla. Mix well.
Pour batter into greased 9x13 inch pan. Bake at 180°C for 30-35 minutes until golden and set.
While cake bakes, make syrup: boil sugar, water, lemon juice, and orange zest for 5 minutes. Let cool.
As soon as cake comes out of oven, score into diamond shapes and pour cooled syrup evenly over hot cake.
Let absorb overnight. Garnish with sliced almonds before serving.

recipes.title: Χαλβάς
recipes.description: Semolina dessert with honey and almonds — simple, comforting, and traditional.
instructions: Toast semolina in a dry pan until lightly golden and fragrant. Set aside.
Make syrup: boil sugar, water, honey, and cinnamon stick for 5 minutes. Keep hot.
In a large pot, heat olive oil or butter. Add toasted semolina and stir constantly 5 minutes.
Carefully pour hot syrup into semolina (it will bubble vigorously). Stir quickly until absorbed.
Mix in toasted almonds and raisins. Pour into a mold or dish. Cool completely before serving.

recipes.title: Κουραμπιέδες
recipes.description: Buttery almond cookies dusted with powdered sugar — a Christmas tradition.
instructions: Cream butter until fluffy. Beat in powdered sugar, vanilla, and brandy or ouzo.
Toast and finely chop almonds. Mix into butter mixture.
Gradually add flour to form a soft dough. Chill 30 minutes.
Form into crescents, balls, or dome shapes. Bake at 160°C for 20 minutes until just set (not browned).
While still warm, dust heavily with powdered sugar. Cool completely and dust again.

recipes.title: Μελομακάρονα
recipes.description: Honey-soaked spice cookies with walnuts — a traditional Greek Christmas treat.
instructions: Mix olive oil, sugar, orange juice, cognac, and orange zest. Beat until creamy.
Add flour, baking powder, cinnamon, cloves, and a pinch of baking soda. Mix to form dough.
Shape into ovals. Place on baking sheets, press gently with fork to flatten slightly.
Bake at 180°C for 25 minutes until golden. Cool 10 minutes.
Dip warm cookies in hot honey syrup (honey, water, sugar, cinnamon). Top with chopped walnuts.

recipes.title: Πορτοκαλόπιτα
recipes.description: Orange phyllo cake soaked in syrup — shredded phyllo creates a unique, crunchy-soft texture.

ingredients.name: dried phyllo dough sheets (from previous day)

instructions: Shred phyllo dough by hand into small pieces. Let dry 1 hour.
Beat eggs with sugar and yogurt. Add orange juice, orange zest, baking powder, and vanilla.
Fold in shredded phyllo. Pour into greased pan.
Bake at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden.
Make syrup: boil sugar, water, and orange juice. Pour cooled syrup over hot cake. Let absorb overnight.

recipes.title: Greek Yogurt Cake
recipes.description: Light and fluffy yogurt cake — simple, versatile, and delicious.
instructions: Beat eggs with sugar until fluffy. Add yogurt, oil, and vanilla.
Sift in flour, baking powder, and a pinch of salt. Mix until smooth.
Pour into greased pan. Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool in pan 10 minutes, then turn out onto wire rack.
Dust with powdered sugar or serve with honey and fruit.

recipes.title: Ρυζόγαλο
recipes.description: Creamy Greek rice pudding with cinnamon — comfort food at its finest.
instructions: Rinse rice. Boil in water for 5 minutes, then drain.
In a pot, heat milk with sugar. Add rice and simmer 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently, until thick and creamy.
Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla or lemon zest.
Pour into bowls. Let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate.
Serve cold, sprinkled with cinnamon.

recipes.title: Τζατζίκι
recipes.description: Cool and refreshing yogurt dip with cucumber, garlic, and dill — the perfect accompaniment to any Greek meal.
instructions: Grate cucumber and squeeze well to remove excess liquid (very important for thick texture).
In a bowl, mix Greek yogurt, squeezed cucumber, crushed garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, and chopped dill.
Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Mix well.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving to allow flavors to blend. Serve cold with pita or as a side.

recipes.title: Σκορδαλιά
recipes.description: Garlic and potato puree with olive oil — a bold and flavorful Greek dip.
instructions: Boil potatoes until very tender. Drain and mash while hot (no lumps).
Add crushed garlic (a lot — this is a garlic dip!), white wine vinegar, and salt.
Slowly drizzle in olive oil while mixing constantly until creamy and emulsified.
Adjust consistency with a bit of potato cooking water if too thick.
Serve at room temperature with fried fish, beets, or bread.

recipes.title: Τυροκαυτερή
recipes.description: Spicy feta and roasted red pepper dip — creamy, zesty, and perfect with pita.
instructions: Roast red peppers until charred. Peel, seed, and chop.
Blend roasted peppers, crumbled feta, Greek yogurt, olive oil, garlic, and red pepper flakes until smooth.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Adjust spice level to taste.
Refrigerate 1 hour to let flavors develop.
Serve cold, drizzled with olive oil and garnished with chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Ντολμάδες
recipes.description: Grape leaves stuffed with aromatic rice, pine nuts, and herbs — a traditional meze.
instructions: If using fresh grape leaves, blanch in boiling water for 2 minutes. If using jarred, rinse well.
Sauté onion in olive oil, add rice and stir for 2 minutes. Add water, pine nuts, raisins, mint, dill, salt, and pepper. Cook 10 minutes.
Place a grape leaf shiny side down. Add a tablespoon of filling to the center, fold sides, and roll tightly.
Arrange rolls seam-side down in a pot. Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, add water to cover.
Place a plate on top to weigh down. Simmer on low heat for 45 minutes. Serve warm or cold.

recipes.title: Φάβα
recipes.description: Smooth yellow split pea puree with olive oil and lemon — a velvety vegetarian dip from Santorini.
instructions: Rinse yellow split peas. Boil with onion and water for 45 minutes until very soft and mushy.
Drain, reserving some cooking liquid. Remove onion.
Blend peas with olive oil, lemon juice, and reserved liquid until smooth and creamy.
Season with salt and white pepper. Adjust texture with more liquid if needed.
Serve warm or at room temperature, topped with capers, chopped onions, and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Μελιτζανοσαλάτα
recipes.description: Smoky roasted eggplant dip with garlic and lemon — simple, flavorful, and addictive.
instructions: Prick eggplants with a fork. Roast over open flame or in 200°C oven until skin is charred and flesh is very soft.
Let cool, then peel off charred skin. Drain eggplant flesh in a colander for 15 minutes.
Mash eggplant with a fork (do not use food processor — texture should be chunky).
Mix in crushed garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Adjust seasoning.
Refrigerate 1 hour. Serve cold, garnished with parsley and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Πατάτες Λεμονάτες
recipes.description: Lemon potatoes roasted in olive oil and herbs — crispy outside, tender inside.
instructions: Cut potatoes into wedges. Place in a baking dish.
Mix lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper. Pour over potatoes.
Add water or broth to 1cm depth.
Bake at 200°C for 1-1.5 hours, turning occasionally, until golden and crispy.
Serve hot as a side dish.

recipes.title: Ρύζι με Άνηθο
recipes.description: Aromatic rice pilaf with fresh dill and lemon — light and flavorful.
instructions: Sauté chopped onion in olive oil until soft.
Add rice and stir for 2 minutes to toast.
Pour in vegetable or chicken broth (2:1 liquid to rice ratio). Bring to boil.
Cover and simmer 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Fluff with a fork. Mix in chopped fresh dill and lemon juice. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Ελιές Μαρινάτες
recipes.description: Greek olives marinated with citrus, herbs, and spices — a classic meze.
instructions: In a jar, combine olives (Kalamata, green, or mixed), olive oil, lemon zest, orange zest, garlic, oregano, and bay leaves.
Add a splash of red wine vinegar and a pinch of red pepper flakes.
Seal jar and shake well. Refrigerate for at least 24 hours.
Bring to room temperature before serving.
Serve as part of a meze platter with bread and cheese.

recipes.title: Μπουγάτσα
recipes.description: Sweet semolina custard wrapped in crispy phyllo — a beloved Greek breakfast pastry.
instructions: Make custard: heat milk with sugar and semolina, stirring constantly until very thick. Remove from heat.
Beat egg yolks and vanilla. Slowly add to warm custard while whisking. Let cool to room temperature.
Brush a baking pan with butter. Layer 4 phyllo sheets, brushing each with melted butter.
Spread custard evenly. Cover with 4 more phyllo sheets, buttering each. Trim edges.
Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until golden. Dust generously with powdered sugar and cinnamon. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Ντάκος
recipes.description: Cretan barley rusk topped with tomatoes, feta, and olive oil — a simple and rustic appetizer.
instructions: Briefly soak barley rusks (paximadia) in water, then drain (they should soften slightly but not dissolve).
Place rusks on a plate. Grate or chop tomatoes and spread over rusks.
Top with crumbled feta, capers, and sliced olives.
Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil. Sprinkle with oregano.
Serve immediately. The rusk should be softened by tomato juices but still have some crunch.

recipes.title: Γίγαντες Πλακί
recipes.description: Giant beans baked in rich tomato sauce with herbs — a hearty vegetarian main dish.
instructions: Soak giant beans overnight. Drain, cover with fresh water, and boil 45 minutes until tender but firm. Drain.
In a baking dish, mix beans with chopped tomatoes, onion, garlic, parsley, olive oil, tomato paste, salt, and pepper.
Add water to barely cover beans. Bake uncovered at 180°C for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Beans should be creamy and sauce thick. If too dry, add water. If too wet, bake longer.
Serve warm or at room temperature, drizzled with olive oil and sprinkled with parsley.

recipes.title: Γιουβέτσι
recipes.description: Beef and orzo baked in tomato sauce — comfort food with a crispy top.

ingredients.name: lamb neck or shoulder cut into pieces

instructions: Brown beef chunks in olive oil. Transfer to a baking dish.
In the same pan, sauté onion and garlic. Add tomato sauce, wine, cinnamon, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes.
Pour sauce over beef. Add water to cover. Cover dish with foil and bake at 160°C for 2 hours.
Add orzo pasta, stir well, add more water if needed. Bake uncovered 30 minutes until orzo is tender and top is crispy.
Let rest 10 minutes. Serve hot with grated cheese.

recipes.title: Χόρτα
recipes.description: Boiled wild greens with lemon and olive oil — simple, healthy, and traditional.

ingredients.name: leafy greens (chard, beet greens, or spinach)

instructions: Clean and rinse greens (dandelion, chicory, or mixed wild greens) thoroughly.
Boil in salted water for 10-15 minutes until tender. Drain well.
Arrange on a platter. Drizzle generously with olive oil and fresh lemon juice.
Season with salt.
Serve warm or at room temperature as a side dish or meze.

recipes.title: Χτυπητή
recipes.description: Spicy feta and roasted red pepper dip — creamy, zesty, and perfect with pita.
instructions: Roast red peppers until charred. Peel, seed, and chop.
Blend roasted peppers, crumbled feta, Greek yogurt, olive oil, garlic, and red pepper flakes until smooth.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Adjust spice level to taste.
Refrigerate 1 hour to let flavors develop.
Serve cold, drizzled with olive oil and garnished with chopped parsley.

recipes.title: Κανταΐφι
recipes.description: Shredded phyllo pastry with nuts and honey syrup — a unique and delicious Greek sweet.
instructions: Mix chopped walnuts, almonds, cinnamon, and sugar.
Take small handfuls of kataifi dough (shredded phyllo), place nut mixture in center, and roll into logs.
Arrange rolls in a buttered baking pan. Brush generously with melted butter.
Bake at 170°C for 40-45 minutes until golden brown.
Pour cooled syrup (honey, sugar, water, lemon) over hot pastry. Let absorb overnight.

recipes.title: Λαδολέμονο
recipes.description: Classic Greek lemon-olive oil dressing — simple, bright, and versatile.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together fresh lemon juice, extra virgin olive oil, crushed garlic, salt, and pepper.
Use a 1:2 ratio of lemon juice to olive oil (adjust to taste).
Whisk vigorously or shake in a jar until emulsified.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
Drizzle over grilled fish, chicken, vegetables, or salads.

recipes.title: Λαγάνα
recipes.description: Flat unleavened bread for Clean Monday — soft, chewy, and traditionally topped with sesame seeds.
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water with sugar. Mix with flour, salt, and olive oil. Knead 10 minutes.
Let rise 1 hour until doubled.
Roll into a large oval or rectangle (about 1cm thick). Place on baking sheet.
Dimple the surface with fingers. Brush with olive oil and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden. Serve fresh with taramosalata and olives.

recipes.title: Φρικασέ Αρνί
recipes.description: Tender lamb stew with romaine lettuce and egg-lemon sauce — a unique Greek comfort dish.
instructions: Brown lamb pieces in olive oil. Add chopped onions and scallions. Cook until soft.
Add water, dill, salt, and pepper. Simmer 1 hour until lamb is tender.
Add chopped romaine lettuce. Cook 15 minutes until wilted.
Beat eggs with lemon juice. Temper with hot broth.
Remove stew from heat, stir in egg-lemon mixture. Serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Σπετσοφάι
recipes.description: Sausage and pepper stew from Pelion — rustic, spicy, and full of flavor.

ingredients.name: assorted bell peppers cut into strips

instructions: Slice Greek sausages (loukaniko) and fry until browned. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté sliced bell peppers, onions, and garlic in olive oil.
Add tomatoes, tomato paste, wine, oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Simmer 15 minutes.
Return sausages to pan. Simmer 10 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve hot with crusty bread or over rice.

recipes.title: Ταραμοσαλάτα
recipes.description: Creamy fish roe dip with lemon and olive oil — a sophisticated meze with a beautiful pink color.

ingredients.name: tarama (salted fish roe — pink or white)
stale white bread without crust, soaked in water

instructions: Soak bread slices in water, then squeeze out excess liquid.
In a food processor, blend tarama (fish roe), soaked bread, and garlic until smooth.
With processor running, slowly drizzle in olive oil and lemon juice alternately until creamy and emulsified.
Adjust seasoning with lemon juice. The dip should be light, fluffy, and pale pink.
Refrigerate 2 hours. Serve cold with warm pita, olives, and cucumber slices.

recipes.title: Ελληνικός Καφές
recipes.description: Traditional Greek coffee brewed in a briki — strong, rich, and aromatic.
instructions: For each cup, add 1 demitasse cup of cold water to a briki (small pot).
Add 1-2 heaping teaspoons of finely ground Greek coffee and sugar to taste (none, medium, or sweet).
Stir well. Heat slowly over low heat without stirring.
Watch carefully. As foam rises to the rim, remove from heat immediately.
Pour into small cups, including the foam (kaimaki). Let grounds settle before drinking. Serve with a glass of cold water.

recipes.title: Ούζο Μεζέ
recipes.description: Ouzo with meze platter — the perfect Greek afternoon ritual.
instructions: Chill ouzo and glasses. Add ice cubes and a splash of cold water to each glass (ouzo will turn cloudy).
Prepare meze platter: arrange olives, feta cheese, tomato slices, cucumber slices, octopus, grilled sardines, and bread.
Add small bowls of tzatziki, taramosalata, and melitzanosalata.
Garnish with lemon wedges and fresh herbs.
Sip ouzo slowly, enjoy meze, and relax in good company.
